intro: Chamaecytisus frivaldszkyanus, also known as Frivaldsky's broom or Hungarian broom, is a perennial shrub native to the Balkan Peninsula and parts of Eastern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Chamaecytisus frivaldszkyanus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
